content.question: What is the independent causal contribution of the 6/8-year track structure itself to Hungary's outlier-level school-based stratification, separate from free school choice, residential segregation, and admissions autonomy?
content.reduction_path: Commission a decomposition analysis (matched-pair school comparisons, instrumental variable study, or regression discontinuity at age 10–11 intake thresholds) comparing students in the 6/8-year track to observably similar students in the standard 8-year comprehensive track, controlling for residential address, parental education, and prior achievement. Replicate in a second European early-selection system (Austria, Germany) to test transportability.
